commands.author: ZOOWH

target.title: fix(telegram): materialize block-boundary newlines in rich HTML for Bot API 10.1
target.body:
## Summary Bot API 10.1 rich messages treat literal newlines between block-level elements as insignificant whitespace, collapsing them instead of rendering line breaks. When the `/status` card (or any multi-line text built with single `\n` separators) is routed through the rich-message path, every field merges into a single run-on line. The root cause is that `convertTelegramRichSegmentNewlines` skips pure-newline segments that hug a structural tag (prev/next block), assuming the block tag itself produces a visual break. This assumption holds in browsers but not in Bot API 10.1 rich messages. Fix: when a segment between two block tags is purely whitespace newlines (no text content) and not inside a rich container (table/figure/details/ul/ol), materialize the newlines as `
` so block boundaries remain visible. Also adds `ul` and `ol` to the rich container tracking set (`TELEGRAM_RICH_LINE_BREAK_CONTAINER_TAGS`) so that pretty-printed list HTML (e.g. `
    \n
  • ŌĆ”
    • \n
`) does not get spurious `
` children injected inside the list container. Fixes #95538 ## Root Cause In `extensions/telegram/src/format.ts`, `convertTelegramRichSegmentNewlines` has `hugsPrev`/`hugsNext` logic that keeps bare `\n` runs literal when they hug a structural tag. This is correct for browsers (block tags produce their own line break) but incorrect for Bot API 10.1 rich messages, where bare newlines between block elements collapse as insignificant whitespace. Additionally, the container tracking set (`TELEGRAM_RICH_LINE_BREAK_CONTAINER_TAGS`) omitted `ul` and `ol`, meaning pretty-printed list HTML could receive unwanted `
` injections inside list containers. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix ## Scope - [x] Integrations ## Real behavior proof **Behavior addressed:** Telegram `/status` card delivered as a single run-on line when `richMessages: true`. Block-boundary newlines between fields collapse. **Environment tested:** Node.js v24.13.1 on Linux, OpenClaw main branch and PR branch. **Before fix (upstream/main ŌĆö bug present):** ``` materializeTelegramRichHtmlLineBreaks("

Plan

\n

Details

") ŌåÆ "

Plan

\n

Details

" ŌåÉ bare \n preserved, collapses in rich messages ``` **After fix (PR branch ŌĆö bug fixed):** ``` materializeTelegramRichHtmlLineBreaks("

Plan

\n

Details

") ŌåÆ "

Plan


Details

" ŌåÉ \n materialized as
, visible in rich messages ``` **Container protection (ul/ol ŌĆö no spurious
):** ``` materializeTelegramRichHtmlLineBreaks("
    \n
  • One
    • \n
  • Two
    • \n
") ŌåÆ "
    \n
  • One
    • \n
  • Two
    • \n
" ŌåÉ newlines preserved inside list container ``` **/status card simulation:** ``` Input: "

Status

\n

Uptime: 39s

\n

Model: claude

" Output: "

Status


Uptime: 39s


Model: claude

" ŌåÆ Each field on its own line Ō£ģ ``` **Pretty-printed list protection:** ``` Input: "
    \n
  • One
    • \n
  • Two
    • \n
" Output: "
    \n
  • One
    • \n
  • Two
    • \n
" ŌåÆ No spurious
inside list Ō£ģ ``` **Supersedes:** #95732 (closed ŌĆö same fix with added ul/ol container protection per ClawSweeper review) ## Checklist - [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) - [x] My changes are covered by tests - [x] I have tested my changes locally
